name: Mount Aso
eruptionHistory: One of the world's most active volcanoes with frequent eruptions. The Naka-dake crater is continuously active. Major caldera-forming eruptions occurred ~90,000 years ago.
description: Mount Aso contains one of the world's largest calderas, measuring 25 km north-south and 18 km east-west. The active Naka-dake crater within the caldera produces frequent phreatic and strombolian eruptions.
